threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to call it Dalston Junction instead of the 'Dalston' everyone else does. Welcome to the local shorthand!
Last weekend, caught a guy singing opera on the 59. Had the whole back row clapping. Only in Brixton does a midnight ride turn into a concert.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw the group of older ladies arguing passionately over the best kebab joint on Hermannstraße, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a currywurst stall open at 3 AM, serving 'Berliner beiden' right next to a techno club. It's a glorious post-party ritual!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name at least three taquerias where the tortillas are made on-site. It’s a game changer.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 Fulton the other day. A street performer turned it into a stage—suddenly, a talent show broke out. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists rave about the Sagrada Família, but they miss our local plazas where Gràcians unwind over vermut, sharing stories that shape our community.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewilderment at the opening hours—weekends in Barcelona are a maze for newcomers still expecting shops to be open all day.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 15-minute wait for your coffee is a scandal. The baristas here expect you to know how to order before you even approach the counter.
Caught a lad on the 208 boasting about his 'life hacks' while papering over his mate’s face with a takeaway box. Classic Bromley night out.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using the U-Bahn platform to practice their breakdancing moves while waiting for the train. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that serves beer on tap. It's like the culinary equivalent of a street art gallery with an absurd twist.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they complain about the Muni delays but still don’t own a car, and they think that’s normal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo get on the 19 Polk, hat in hand. He just smiled and said, 'Going to a wedding, wish me luck!' Muni’s magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the plazas are for gathering, but locals use them just to pass through. We avoid the crowds, and the benches are often empty.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their bewilderment staring at the metro map, trying to find the perfect route to Park Güell. Been there, mate.
